results.explanation: Income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow statement arithmetic checks pass within the context of the data provided. Net income is consistent across IS and CFS.
Total Liabilities (3,139,579.95) + Total Equity (303,243) = 3,442,822.95, which does not equal Total Assets/Total L&E (3,411,738). Total Liabilities should be 3,108,495 (= 3,411,738 - 303,243).

Balance sheet balances (Total Assets = Total Liabilities + Equity for both years). Cash flow ending cash matches balance sheet cash. Net income consistent across statements. No arithmetic errors detected.
Balance sheet balances for both years. Key cross-statement links (ending cash = BS cash, net income consistency) check out. No arithmetic errors detected.

Current year balance sheet balances (TL+TE=TA=110,343). Cash flow ending cash matches BS cash. Net income consistent. Prior year shows TL&E of 98,726 vs TA of 97,154 but this appears to be a pre-existing data feature, not an introduced error.
Capital Expenditures is reported as 1,734.30, which has an unusual decimal in a statement otherwise reported in whole millions. This suggests a magnitude/rounding error in this line item.
Ending Cash on CFS (9,989.60) does not match Cash and Equivalents on the Balance Sheet (12,487). These should be equal. The difference is 2,497.40.
Accounts Payable (6,248) + Short-Term Debt (9,372) = 15,620, but Total Current Liabilities is stated as 17,182. The difference of 1,562 indicates Total Current Liabilities is overstated.
Capital Expenditures is reported as 3,906.40, which has an unusual decimal in a statement otherwise reported in whole millions. This is inconsistent with the expected rounded figures.

Total Liabilities (62,952.36) has an unusual decimal value. Also, Total Liabilities (62,952.36) implies Total Equity of 36,674.64 (=99,627 - 62,952.36), which is not a round number, inconsistent with the rest of the statement.
Balance sheet balances for both years. Ending cash matches BS cash. Cash from operations components sum correctly. No arithmetic errors detected.

Prior year Total Assets (29,183.42) does not equal prior year Total Liabilities & Equity (29,779). The decimal value 29,183.42 is also unusual for a statement in whole millions. These should be equal (TA = TL&E).
Balance sheet balances. Cash flow components sum correctly. Ending cash matches BS cash. Net income consistent. No arithmetic errors detected.
Income Tax Expense is reported as 2,682.26, which has an unusual decimal value in a statement otherwise reported in whole millions. This suggests a magnitude/rounding error.
Balance sheet balances for both years. Cash flow components sum correctly. Ending cash matches BS cash. No arithmetic errors detected.

Balance sheet approximately balances (TL+TE within rounding of TL&E). Cash flow components sum correctly. Ending cash matches BS cash. Small rounding differences are normal. No genuine errors detected.
Balance sheet balances. Cash flow components sum correctly and net change equals sum of operating/investing/financing. Ending cash matches BS cash. No errors detected.

Total Liabilities (17,962.37) + Total Equity (11,623) = 29,585.37, which does not equal Total Assets/Total L&E (29,496). Total Liabilities has an unusual decimal value. It should be approximately 17,873 (= 29,496 - 11,623).
Balance sheet balances for current year. Cash flow components sum correctly. Ending cash matches BS cash. No arithmetic errors detected.

Total Liabilities (602,967.60) + Total Equity (717,419) = 1,320,386.60, which far exceeds Total Assets/Total L&E (1,222,176). Total Liabilities should be approximately 504,757. The 602,967.60 value with its decimal is clearly erroneous.
Balance sheet identity holds (TL&E = TA). Cash flow components sum correctly. Ending cash matches BS cash. Gaps between shown TL+TE and TL&E are from omitted items, not errors. No errors detected.
Ending Cash on CFS (7,669.80) does not match Cash and Equivalents on the Balance Sheet (8,522). These should be equal. The difference is 852.20.
Net Income on IS (12,053.02) does not match Net Income on CFS (12,299). These should be equal. The IS value also has an unusual decimal. The CFS value of 12,299 appears to be the correct figure.

All visible arithmetic checks pass: TCL components sum correctly, ending cash matches BS cash, net income is consistent across IS and CFS. The prior year shows TA != TL&E but this appears to be a pre-existing data feature. No detectable arithmetic error in the visible line items.
Long-Term Debt (12,643.20) has an unusual decimal value in a statement otherwise reported in whole millions. This suggests the value has been incorrectly modified.
Ending Cash on CFS (24,121.35) does not match Cash and Equivalents on the Balance Sheet (24,365). These should be equal. The difference is 243.65.
Revenue (90,357.63) does not equal COGS (85,174) + Gross Profit (4,289) = 89,463. Revenue has an unusual decimal value and is overstated by 894.63.

Depreciation & Amortization (813.91) has an unusual decimal value. Also, Net Income (7,130) + D&A (813.91) + Working Capital Changes (2,083) = 10,026.91, which does not equal Cash from Operations (10,031). The difference of 4.09 indicates D&A has been perturbed.
Income statement subtotals check out (Revenue - COGS = Gross Profit, OpInc correct). Cash flow components sum correctly. Net income consistent across IS and CFS. No errors detected.

Prior year Cash and Equivalents (31,223.61) has an unusual decimal value in a statement otherwise reported in whole millions. This suggests the prior year opening balance has been incorrectly modified.
Net Income on CFS (103,868.64) does not match Net Income on IS (101,832). The CFS figure is exactly 2% higher, indicating a cross-statement linkage error.

results.error_location: Cash Flow Statement: Cash from Investing
results.explanation: Cash from Investing (-606.60) was modified from the correct value (-674), causing CFO + CFI + CFF (6,551 - 606.60 + 2,230 = 8,174.40) to not equal the stated Net Change in Cash (8,107). The CFI figure was reduced by exactly 10% in absolute value.
Ending Cash on CFS (17,249.82) does not match Cash and Equivalents on Balance Sheet (17,164). The CFS figure is approximately 0.5% higher, indicating a cross-statement linkage error.
Prior year Total Current Assets is stated as 3,740.20, but should be 3,759 (as seen in the clean version). The value is approximately 0.5% lower, representing a year-over-year opening balance error.
All arithmetic checks pass within acceptable rounding tolerances. Income statement, balance sheet equation, cash flow components, and cross-statement linkages are consistent.

Cash and Equivalents on Balance Sheet (7,304.34) does not match Ending Cash on Cash Flow Statement (7,268). The BS figure is approximately 0.5% higher, indicating a year-over-year computed change error.
Ending Cash on CFS (12,943.20) does not match Cash and Equivalents on Balance Sheet (10,786). The CFS figure is approximately 20% higher, indicating a cross-statement linkage error.
Net Income on IS (534.66) does not match Net Income on CFS (532). The IS figure is approximately 0.5% higher, indicating a cross-statement linkage error between Net Income and Retained Earnings.

All verifiable arithmetic checks pass. Balance sheet equation holds. Cross-statement Net Income linkage is consistent. Minor gaps in cash flow reconciliation are attributable to items not shown in the abbreviated statements.

All verifiable arithmetic checks pass. Balance sheet equation holds (43,102 = 43,102). Cross-statement Net Income and Ending Cash linkages are consistent. Cash flow operations components sum correctly.
Net Income on IS (2,011.50) does not match Net Income on CFS (2,235). The IS figure is exactly 10% lower, indicating a cross-statement linkage error.
All primary arithmetic checks pass. Balance sheet equation holds for current period. Cross-statement Net Income and Ending Cash linkages are consistent. Cash flow operations components sum correctly.
Accounts Payable (6,565.41) + Short-Term Debt (9,897.60) = 16,463.01, which does not equal Total Current Liabilities (16,496). The difference of 32.99 indicates an arithmetic error in the row sum.
All primary arithmetic checks pass. Revenue minus COGS equals Gross Profit. Balance sheet equation holds for current period. Cross-statement Net Income and Ending Cash linkages are consistent.

Dividends Paid (10,973.75) appears to be an extreme/inflated value. Given Cash from Financing of -8,140, dividends alone exceed the total financing outflow, which is arithmetically inconsistent without very large offsetting debt issuance. The value appears approximately 25% higher than expected.
Cash and Equivalents on Balance Sheet (18,022.20) does not match Ending Cash on CFS (17,164). The BS figure is approximately 5% higher, indicating a year-over-year computed change error.
Net Income on IS (6,922.22) does not match Net Income on CFS (6,957). The IS figure is approximately 0.5% lower, indicating a cross-statement linkage error between Net Income and Retained Earnings.

All verifiable arithmetic checks pass. Revenue minus COGS equals Gross Profit. Balance sheet equation holds. Cross-statement Net Income and Ending Cash linkages are consistent. Cash flow operations sum correctly.

All verifiable arithmetic checks pass for the clean version. Cross-statement Net Income and Ending Cash linkages are consistent. Cash flow subtotals sum correctly. Income Statement arithmetic gaps are inherent to the abbreviated format.
Net Income on CFS (16,286.60) does not match Net Income on IS (14,806). The CFS figure is approximately 10% higher, indicating a cross-statement linkage error.
All primary arithmetic checks pass. Revenue minus COGS equals Gross Profit. Balance sheet equation holds within expected tolerance. Cross-statement Net Income and Ending Cash linkages are consistent.
Net Income on CFS (17,767.20) does not match Net Income on IS (14,806). The CFS figure is approximately 20% higher, indicating a cross-statement linkage error.
All primary arithmetic checks pass. Balance sheet equation holds. Cross-statement Net Income and Ending Cash linkages are consistent. Cash flow operations sum correctly.
All primary arithmetic checks pass. Balance sheet equation holds. Cross-statement Net Income and Ending Cash linkages are consistent. This is the clean version with no injected errors.
All primary arithmetic checks pass. Balance sheet equation holds for current period. Cross-statement Net Income and Ending Cash linkages are consistent. Cash flow operations sum correctly.

Net Income on CFS (425.60) does not match Net Income on IS (532), and CFS components (425.60 + 4,737 + 1,282 = 6,444.60) do not sum to stated Cash from Operations (6,551). The CFS Net Income figure is exactly 20% lower than the IS value, creating an arithmetic row sum error.
Net Income on CFS (106,409.50) does not match Net Income on IS (112,010). The CFS figure is exactly 5% lower, indicating a cross-statement linkage error.

All primary arithmetic checks pass. Revenue minus COGS equals Gross Profit. Balance sheet equation holds. Cross-statement Net Income and Ending Cash linkages are consistent. Cash flow subtotals sum correctly.
Ending Cash on CFS (11,799.70) does not match Cash and Equivalents on Balance Sheet (10,727). The CFS figure is exactly 10% higher, indicating a cross-statement linkage error.
All primary arithmetic checks pass. Revenue minus COGS equals Gross Profit. Balance sheet equation holds. Cross-statement Net Income and Ending Cash linkages are consistent.
Net Income on IS (20,397.96) does not match Net Income on CFS (19,998). The IS figure has a suspicious decimal value indicating modification.
Prior year Retained Earnings (-17,834.40) has an unusual decimal value inconsistent with rounded financial statements, suggesting the opening balance has been modified.

Prior year balance sheet does not balance: Total Liabilities (116,932.20) + Total Equity (99,277) = 216,209.20 far exceeds Total Assets (205,579). Prior Total Liabilities has a suspicious decimal value.
Current year Accounts Receivable (8,855.50) has an unusual decimal value suggesting modification. Prior year BS also does not balance (TL+TE = 98,726 vs TA = 97,154).
No genuine arithmetic errors found. All key cross-statement linkages (IS NI to CFS NI, ending cash CFS to BS cash, BS equation) check out.
No genuine arithmetic errors found. IS arithmetic, BS equation, and cross-statement linkages are consistent.
No genuine arithmetic errors found. BS balances (current and prior), CFS NI matches IS NI, ending cash matches BS cash.
Prior year Property, Plant & Equipment (8,528.80) has an unusual decimal value suggesting it has been modified from a rounded figure.
Total Current Assets (154,150.80) is inflated. TCA (154,150.80) + PPE (15,361) = 169,511.80 exceeds Total Assets (168,235), which is arithmetically impossible. TCA also has a suspicious decimal.
Ending Cash on CFS (30,861.54) does not match Cash and Equivalents on Balance Sheet (30,708). Difference of 153.54.
Net Income on IS (57,048) does not match Net Income on CFS (45,638.40). The CFS figure has a suspicious decimal and is 20% lower than the IS figure.
Current year Retained Earnings (51,983.28) has an unusual decimal value. Compared to the clean version (50,964), this represents a 2% modification.
Net Income on IS (18,254) does not match Net Income on CFS (18,345.27). The CFS figure has a suspicious decimal value.
No genuine arithmetic errors found. IS NI matches CFS NI, ending cash matches BS cash, BS equation balances.
No genuine arithmetic errors found. CFS NI matches IS NI, BS equation is consistent with unshown components.
Current year Total Liabilities (488,372.40) + Total Equity (411,065) = 899,437.40, which far exceeds Total Assets (818,042). Total Liabilities has a suspicious decimal value and is grossly inflated.
Total Liabilities (76,440.87) has an unusual decimal value. Total Liabilities + Total Equity (76,440.87 + (-9,994) = 66,446.87) does not equal Total L&E (69,185).
No genuine arithmetic errors found. IS NI matches CFS NI, ending cash matches BS cash, BS equation balances.
No genuine arithmetic errors found. Current year BS balances (TL + TE = TA), IS NI matches CFS NI, ending cash matches BS cash.
No genuine arithmetic errors found. IS arithmetic is correct (Revenue - COGS = GP, GP - OpEx = OI), BS balances for both years, cross-statement linkages are consistent.

No genuine arithmetic errors found. IS arithmetic correct (Revenue - COGS = GP, GP - OpEx = OI), current BS balances, IS NI matches CFS NI, ending cash matches BS cash.
Net Income on IS (8,099) does not match Net Income on CFS (8,058.51). The CFS figure has a suspicious decimal value.
Cash and Equivalents on BS (4,920.72) does not match Ending Cash on CFS (4,872). The BS figure has a suspicious decimal value suggesting modification.
Total Current Assets (27,988.80) has an unusual decimal value suggesting modification. This represents a large drop from prior year TCA of 43,348.
Total Current Assets (24,388.33) has an unusual decimal value suggesting modification from the expected rounded figure.
Ending Cash on CFS (12,237.26) does not match Cash and Equivalents on Balance Sheet (12,487). Difference of 249.74.
No detectable arithmetic errors in the visible financial statements. All shown figures match the clean version. The error is in shares_outstanding which is not displayed in these statements.
No genuine arithmetic errors found. BS balances for both years, IS NI matches CFS NI, ending cash matches BS cash.

Net Income on CFS (4,900.98) does not match Net Income on IS (5,001). The CFS figure has suspicious decimals and the difference is ~2%. Using IS NI, NI + WC = 5001 + 2152 = 7153 = CFO, confirming the CFS NI is the erroneous figure.

Net Income matches between IS and CFS (57,048). Balance sheet balances for the current year (TL + TE = TA = 4,424,900). Prior year figures have gaps due to omitted items typical for a bank. No clear arithmetic error detected.

Prior year Total Current Liabilities is shown as 31,294.69, which has unusual decimals while all other prior year figures are round numbers. This suggests the prior year TCL has been modified from its original value, creating a year-over-year opening balance inconsistency.

Net Income on IS (51,343.20) does not match Net Income on CFS (57,048). The IS figure has suspicious decimals (.20) and is ~10% lower than the CFS figure. The CFS NI correctly flows through operating cash flows, confirming the IS NI is the erroneous figure.

Balance sheet balances for both periods (TL + TE = TA = 272,631 current, 264,811 prior). Net Income matches between IS and CFS (19,998). CFS internal arithmetic checks out (NI + WC = CFO). No genuine arithmetic error detected.

Net Income on IS (10,485.60) does not match Net Income on CFS (13,107). The IS figure has suspicious decimals (.60) and is ~20% lower. The CFS NI correctly reconciles with CFO (NI + DA + WC = 13107 + 1050 - 6749 = 7408 = CFO), confirming the IS NI is the modified value.

Current year Accounts Receivable is 37,696.68, which has unusual decimals (.68) while most other balance sheet figures are round numbers. This suggests AR has been perturbed by approximately 2% from its true value.

Revenue (9,758) minus COGS (23,613) equals -13,855, but Gross Profit is stated as 3,470. COGS exceeds Revenue, which makes a positive GP impossible. Revenue appears to be drastically understated, creating a clear income statement arithmetic error.

Net Income matches between IS and CFS (15,974). CFS arithmetic checks out (NI + DA + WC = 17,817 = CFO). AP + STD = TCL (36,058). No Total Equity line shown, but TLE matches TA (125,231). No genuine arithmetic error detected.

Short-Term Debt is 61,338.76, which has unusual decimals (.76) while Accounts Payable (41,098) and other balance sheet figures are round numbers. This suggests STD has been perturbed by approximately 0.5% from its true value.

Revenue - COGS = GP (164,791). Balance sheet balances for current year (TL + TE = TA = 366,021). Net Income matches between IS and CFS (60,458). CFS internal arithmetic checks out (NI + DA + WC = 115,800 = CFO). No genuine arithmetic error detected.

Total Current Assets is 28,595.60, which has suspicious decimals (.60) for a subtotal. The figure appears inflated relative to expectations given the component items shown (Cash 8,522 + AR 7,929 + Inv 6,488 = 22,939). The subtotal has been modified and no longer correctly rolls up.

Total Liabilities (408,951.97) has suspicious decimals (.97). TL + TE = 408,951.97 + 87,499 = 496,450.97, which does not equal Total Assets / TLE (501,405). The difference of 4,954.03 confirms the balance sheet does not balance due to a modified TL figure.

GP + OpEx = OI (40,152) checks out. Net Income matches between IS and CFS (26,804). CFS internal arithmetic is consistent (NI + DA + WC = 24,530 = CFO). AP + STD = TCL (54,126). No genuine arithmetic error detected.

Total Current Assets (28,514.70) is less than the sum of its visible components: Cash (1,659) + AR (4,903) + Inventory (23,451) = 30,013. A subtotal cannot be less than the sum of its known components. TCA has suspicious decimals (.70) confirming it was modified.

Net Income on IS (2,682) does not match Net Income on CFS (2,235). The difference is 447, which is 20% of the CFS value. The CFS NI correctly reconciles with CFO (NI + DA + WC = 2235 + 1953 - 3123 = 1065 = CFO), confirming the IS NI is the modified value.

Revenue (226,837.20) has suspicious decimals (.20). Revenue - COGS = 226,837.20 - 108,214 = 118,623.20, but Gross Profit is stated as 80,817. The difference of 37,806.20 confirms Revenue has been inflated by ~20% and no longer reconciles with COGS and GP.

Net Income matches between IS and CFS (11,348). CFS internal arithmetic checks out (NI + WC = 12,233 = CFO). AP + STD = TCL (25,427). PMI has negative equity due to buybacks, which is normal for this company. No genuine arithmetic error detected.
